content.cn_text: 你将听到两个商学专业的学生在讨论他们将要做的一篇关于团队有效工作的文章。
content.en_text: You will hear two business studies students discussing a presentation they'll do on an article on working effectively in groups.

content.cn_text: 布拉德，你觉得那篇关于小组合作的文章怎么样?
content.en_text: So, Brad, what did you think of the article on group work?

content.cn_text: 哦，嗨，海伦……是的，非常好……关于如何使小组工作有效的一些有用的建议。
content.en_text: Oh hi, Helen ... yeah it was pretty good ... with helpful pieces of advice on how to make group work effective.

content.cn_text: 我认为我们很幸运能在管理技能研讨会上得到如此简单易懂的文本。
content.en_text: I think we were lucky to be given such a straightforward text to present at the management skills seminar.

content.cn_text: 是的…实际上，我们现在讨论一下……你有时间吗?
content.en_text: Yeah ... actually shall we discuss it now ... have you got time?

content.cn_text: 确定……这只是一个十分钟的演讲，所以我们只需要解释一下，然后就文章中提出的要点发表我们的观点。
content.en_text: Sure ... it's only a ten-minute presentation, so we just need to explain and then give our views on the main points raised in the article.

content.cn_text: 我要做些笔记……正确的。
content.en_text: I'll jot down some notes ... Right.

content.cn_text: 有三个主要部分。我建议我们从倾听开始。
content.en_text: So, there are three main sections. I suggest we start with listening.

content.cn_text: 是的…有效的小组倾听……因为这不是我们这个领域课程中经常涉及的内容……
content.en_text: Yeah ... effective listening in groups ... because it's not something that's frequently covered on courses in our field ...

content.cn_text: 不…我们应该在演讲中提到。
content.en_text: No ... and we should say that in the presentation.

content.cn_text: 是的…而且有效的倾听很简单，你知道，我认为这并不难学。嗯…人们认为这很容易，但根据我的经验，我们大多数人往往是非常懒惰的听众。好吧——我不反对这一点。
content.en_text: Yeah ... And also effective listening's pretty simple, you know, I don't think it's hard to learn. Well ... people think it's easy, but in my experience most of us tend to be very lazy listeners. OK - I wouldn't argue with that.

content.cn_text: 我确实认为我们应该强调的是听者的姿势、手势等在使说话者感到被尊重方面的作用。
content.en_text: Something I do think we should emphasise is the power of the listener's posture,gestures, etc. in making speakers feel respected.

content.cn_text: …并不是说你只是在等他们做完。在你提出自己的想法之前……
content.en_text: ... not that you're just waiting for them to finish.before jumping in with your own ideas ...

content.cn_text: 啊哈。
content.en_text: Ah ha.

content.cn_text: 对吧……下一部分是关于目标设定——让我们确保我们清楚这篇文章是怎么说的。
content.en_text: Right ... the next section is on goal setting - let's make sure we're clear what the article says on this.

content.cn_text: 是的，首先它说所有的小组成员必须有时间解释他们自己的目标。
content.en_text: Yeah - well, firstly it says that all group members must be given time to explain their own goals.

content.cn_text: …就是这样。
content.en_text: ... that's it, yeah.

content.cn_text: …然后它有没有说整个团队应该就共同目标达成一致?
content.en_text: ... and then did it say that the whole group should agree on common goals?

content.cn_text: 那有点太浓了。更重要的是，每个人的议程都应该平等地被接受……但它确实说目标必须是现实的，你知道.....可在一年内完成
content.en_text: That's a bit too strong. It's more that everyone's agendas should be equally acceptable ...but it does say that goals have to be realistic, you know ..... achievable within a

content.cn_text: 特定的时间吗?
content.en_text: particular time?

content.cn_text: 你说对了。这篇文章就是这么说的。如果团队成员知道他们不会在合理的时间内取得任何成果，那么拥有“理想”真的没有任何意义。
content.en_text: You've got it. That's really what the article's saying. There isn't really any point in having'ideals' if group members know they won't come to anything within a reasonable period ...

content.cn_text: 所以…我认为对这部分的演讲来说，一个涵盖这些要点的总结就足够了，你觉得呢?
content.en_text: So ... I think a summary covering those points will be enough for that part of the presentation, don't you?

content.cn_text: 是的……最后一部分是关于解决冲突。
content.en_text: Yep ... Now the last section is about conflict resolution.

content.cn_text: 事实上，我认为这是文章中最糟糕的部分。
content.en_text: Actually, I thought it was the worst part of the article.

content.cn_text: 我也是。
content.en_text: Me too .

content.cn_text: 我认为它在这个问题上没有讲得足够详细。
content.en_text: I don't think it went into sufficient detail on the issue.

content.cn_text: 事实上，我认为它占用了太多的空间，但是你知道，它非常无聊。
content.en_text: Actually I thought it devoted too much space to it but that it was all rather boring, you know.

content.cn_text: 它没有提到一些更激进的理论……当然，我觉得那很烦人。
content.en_text: It didn't mention some of the more radical theories ... absolutely .I found that really irritating.

content.cn_text: 对吧……而且我认为它还可以更多地说明冲突有时在群体中是健康的……
content.en_text: Right ... and also I think it could have said more about conflict sometimes being healthy in groups ...

content.cn_text: 绝对……它只是相当委婉地提到我们应该如何避免考虑赢家和输家，以及快速解决冲突总是可取的。
content.en_text: Absolutely ... it just mentioned rather glibly about how we should avoid thinking of winners and losers and that quick resolution of conflict is always desirable.

content.cn_text: 布拉德:不解释这些术语的意思……？
content.en_text: Brad: Without explaining what these terms mean ... ?

content.cn_text: 海伦:嗯，它给出了非常详细的定义，但是没有形成一个适当的论点。
content.en_text: Helen: Well, it gives quite detailed definitions but doesn't develop a proper argument.

content.cn_text: 布拉德:对……所以在演讲中，我想我们只需要给出一些定义和…
content.en_text: Brad: Right ... So for the presentation, I think we just give some definitions and ...

content.cn_text: 海伦:……然后解释一下我们认为这篇文章在处理冲突方面的弱点
content.en_text: Helen: ... and then explain what we felt were the weaknesses in the article's treatment of conflict

content.cn_text: 决议。
content.en_text: resolution.

content.cn_text: 布拉德:是啊……好。
content.en_text: Brad: Yeah ... good.

content.cn_text: 布莱德:那么，让我们想想我们要为实际的演示准备些什么。
content.en_text: Brad: So, let's think about what we have to prepare for the actual presentation.

content.cn_text: 海伦:嗯，我想我们会用ppt……但我不太会用，尤其是如果它有视觉效果的话。我真的得考虑上一门有关它的课程，因为我知道我将来会需要它的。
content.en_text: Helen: Well, I suppose we'll use PowerPoint ... but I'm hopeless at using it, especially if it has any visuals. I really have to look into doing a course on it because I know I'll need it in the future.

content.cn_text: 布莱德:别担心，我很喜欢用ppt，等其他东西都准备好了我会把它放在一起的。
content.en_text: Brad: Don't worry, I'm quite happy using PowerPoint and I'll put it together when everything else is ready.

content.cn_text: 海伦:那我就放心了……但是，是的，稍后再做。
content.en_text: Helen: That's a relief ... but, yes, do that later.

content.cn_text: 布莱德:好吧。现在，我听到导师说我们必须从文章中精选一些引文。
content.en_text: Brad: OK. Now, I heard the tutor saying we have to include some well chosen quotations from the article?

content.cn_text: 海伦:我不确定我们是否……我会给他发邮件问问。
content.en_text: Helen: I'm not sure if we do ... I'll email him to find out.

content.cn_text: 布莱德:不需要，我可以看看他设定任务时给我们的规格……
content.en_text: Brad: No need, I can just have a look at the specs he gave us when he set the task ...

content.cn_text: 海伦:那样会快一些。
content.en_text: Helen: That'll be quicker.

content.cn_text: 布拉德:但是导师明确地说我们必须准备一份讲义来配合演讲……我不知道该怎么做。
content.en_text: Brad: But the tutor definitely said we have to prepare a handout to go with the talk ...I'm not really sure how we do that.

content.cn_text: 海伦:莎拉去年做了一个……
content.en_text: Helen: Sarah did one last year ...

content.cn_text: 布拉德:她是谁?
content.en_text: Brad: Who's she?

content.cn_text: 海伦:她在市场营销方面的选择和我一样。我会征求她的意见，告诉她该写些什么。
content.en_text: Helen: She's doing the same option as me on marketing. I'll ask her advice on what to include.

content.cn_text: 布莱德:好了。所以最后只剩下参考书目了。我想主要是文章吧。
content.en_text: Brad: Great. So that just leaves the bibliography at the end. I suppose it'll mainly be articles.

content.cn_text: 海伦:是的。所以我们就上网看看…我们可以稍后再谈。
content.en_text: Helen: Yeah. So we'll just look on the web ... and we can leave that till later.

content.cn_text: 布拉德:但是有人建议我们不要那样做……
content.en_text: Brad: But we've been advised against that ...

content.cn_text: 海伦:嗯，我们可以看看图书馆的期刊。
content.en_text: Helen: Well, we could have a look through some journals in the library.

content.cn_text: 布莱德:我想我们应该先看看模块手册。我想这会给我们一些好线索。
content.en_text: Brad: I think we should start by looking through module handbooks. I think that'll give us some good leads.

content.cn_text: 海伦:是啊……你可能是对的。所以，这就是所有的…
content.en_text: Helen: Yeah ... you're probably right. So, that's all the ...

groups.title: What TWO things do Brad and Helen agree to say about listening in groups?

collect_options.title: What TWO things do Brad and Helen agree to say about listening in groups?

list.title: A
list.content: Listening skills are often overlooked in business training.

list.title: B
list.content: Learning to listen well is a skill that's easy for most people to learn.

list.title: C
list.content: It's sometimes acceptable to argue against speakers.

list.title: D
list.content: Body language is very important when listening.

list.title: E
list.content: Listeners should avoid interrupting speakers.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A和D，因为原文中明确提到了这两点是Brad和Helen同意在演讲中要提到的。A选项对应原文中提到的"不经常在他们领域的课程中涉及"，这与"经常被忽视"的含义相符。D选项则直接对应原文中提到的听者的姿势和手势的重要性。这两点都是他们在讨论中明确同意要强调的内容。
这道题的正确答案是A和D，因为原文中明确提到了这两点是Brad和Helen同意在演讲中要提到的。A选项对应原文中提到的"不经常在他们领域的课程中涉及"，这与"经常被忽视"的含义相符。D选项则直接对应原文中提到的听者的姿势和手势的重要性。这两点都是他们在讨论中明确同意要强调的内容。

groups.title: What TWO things does the article say about goal-setting?

collect_options.title: What TWO things does the article say about goal-setting?

list.title: A
list.content: Meetings should start with a clear statement of goals.

list.title: B
list.content: It's important for each individual's goals to be explained.

list.title: C
list.content: Everybody in the group should have the same goals.

list.title: D
list.content: Goals should be a mix of the realistic and the ideal.

list.title: E
list.content: Goals must always to be achievable within a set time.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B和E。文章强调了两个关于目标设定的重要观点：首先，每个小组成员都应该有机会解释自己的目标，这直接对应了选项B。其次，文章指出目标应该在合理的时间内可以实现，这与选项E的描述相符。这两点都是文章中明确提到的关于目标设定的重要观点，因此是正确答案。
这道题的正确答案是B和E。文章强调了两个关于目标设定的重要观点：首先，每个小组成员都应该有机会解释自己的目标，这直接对应了选项B。其次，文章指出目标应该在合理的时间内可以实现，这与选项E的描述相符。这两点都是文章中明确提到的关于目标设定的重要观点，因此是正确答案。

groups.title: What TWO things do Brad and Helen agree are weak points in the article's section on conflict resolution?

collect_options.title: What TWO things do Brad and Helen agree are weak points in the article's section on conflict resolution?

list.title: A
list.content: It doesn't explore the topic in enough detail.

list.title: B
list.content: It only discusses conservative views.

list.title: C
list.content: It says nothing about the potential value of conflict.

list.title: D
list.content: It talks too much about 'winners and losers'.

list.title: E
list.content: It doesn't provide definitions of key terms.

items.analysis: 文章中Brad和Helen讨论了文章关于冲突解决部分的弱点。他们认为文章忽视了更激进的理论，这表明文章只讨论了保守的观点（选项B）。同时，他们指出文章没有充分讨论冲突在团队中可能带来的积极作用（选项C）。这两点恰好对应了选项B和C，因此这两个选项是正确答案。
文章中Brad和Helen讨论了文章关于冲突解决部分的弱点。他们认为文章忽视了更激进的理论，这表明文章只讨论了保守的观点（选项B）。同时，他们指出文章没有充分讨论冲突在团队中可能带来的积极作用（选项C）。这两点恰好对应了选项B和C，因此这两个选项是正确答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What actions do Brad and Helen agree to do regarding the following preparation tasks?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to the number.
Preparation tasks

list.title: A
list.content: Contact the tutor for clarification.

list.title: B
list.content: Check the assignment specifications.

list.title: C
list.content: Leave it until the last task.

list.title: D
list.content: Ask a course-mate to help.

list.title: E
list.content: Find information on the Internet.

list.title: F
list.content: Look through course handbooks.

items.title: Preparing the powerpoint
items.analysis: 正确答案是C（Leave it until the last task），因为Brad说他会在"everything else is ready"之后制作PowerPoint。这表明PowerPoint的准备被安排在所有其他任务之后，即作为最后一项任务来完成。这种安排与选项C的描述完全吻合。

items.title: Using direct quotations
items.analysis: 正确答案是B（Check the assignment specifications），因为Brad提出直接查看任务规格说明来确认引用要求。虽然Helen最初提议给导师发邮件询问，但Brad随后提出了一个更直接、更有效的解决方案。这表明他们最终决定通过查看任务规格来解决关于直接引用的问题，而不是联系导师或采取其他行动。

items.title: Creating a handout
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D（Ask a course-mate to help），因为Helen提到她会向同学Sarah询问关于handout的建议。Sarah是Helen的同学（course-mate），而且Helen明确表示要向她寻求帮助（"I'll ask her advice"）。这正好符合选项D的描述，即向同学寻求帮助。

items.title: Drawing up a bibliography
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是F（Look through course handbooks），因为在讨论如何准备参考文献时，Brad建议从查阅课程手册开始，并认为这会提供一些好的线索。Helen同意了这个建议，说"Yeah ... you're probably right"。这表明他们最终达成了共识，决定通过查阅课程手册来开始准备参考文献。
